title: cryptocarya putida
intro: Cryptocarya putida, also known as the 'Makamaka' tree, is a species of plant in the laurel family that is native to the tropical forests of Hawaii. The leaves and bark of the Cryptocarya putida plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, containing medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of the plant include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues. The leaves and bark can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
